title: eclipta alba var. prostrata
intro: Eclipta alba var. prostrata, also known as Eclipta or Bhringaraj, is a herbaceous plant native to North America, characterized by its creeping stems and small yellow flowers. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, promote wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Eclipta alba var. prostrata include its potential to promote hair growth, improve cognitive function, and support liver health, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. Eclipta alba var. prostrata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs in herbal remedies.
